project: Probabilistic Deep Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Risk Analysis
objective: Maximise expected risk-adjusted return subject to a drawdown-floor capital-preservation constraint, evaluated against three named comparators (passive buy-and-hold, rule-based trailing stop-loss, baseline PPO with no uncertainty signal) on a fully reproducible protocol.

named_groups_notes.fiyins_portfolio: Live holdings as of April 2026: 41 single-name equities and 29 ETFs across the Yochaa and Bamboo brokerage accounts. (The earlier 42-ticker watchlist split has been collapsed into a single live-holdings group now that all watchlist positions have been opened.)
named_groups_notes.fiyins_stocks: The 41 single-name equities subset of fiyins_portfolio; useful for stock-only analyses.
named_groups_notes.fiyins_etfs: The 29 ETF holdings subset of fiyins_portfolio; useful for ETF-only analyses.

ticker_normalisations.DIA: Listed as 'DJI' in source; DJI is a non-tradable index, DIA is the corresponding SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF.
ticker_normalisations.BRK-B: Berkshire Hathaway Class B; the retail-tradable share class.
ticker_normalisations.EEMX: Listed as 'SPYM/EEMX' in source; SPYM is not a valid ticker, EEMX (iShares ESG MSCI EM) is.
ticker_normalisations.skipped: IBBQ — not a recognised ticker; skipped from the source list.

walk_forward_folds.notes: Includes COVID flash crash (Feb-Mar 2020) and recovery rally.
Includes 2022 bear market and 2023 rate-hike cycle.

training_budget.notes: Use 'smoke' for quick CPU smoke tests, 'full' for the headline reruns. The legacy 'baseline.timesteps' and 'probabilistic_agent.timesteps' fields below are kept for backwards compatibility with existing runners.

bootstrap.notes: Politis & Romano (1994) stationary block bootstrap; resamples blocks of historical log returns to expand the effective training set without leaving the empirical return distribution.
